channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Even as War Rages, Investors Bet Oil Will Be Cheaper in a Few Months
item.description: Futures contracts reflect an expectation that low inventories and midterm elections are likely to push the Trump administration to try to ease tensions with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Joe Rennison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: An oil pump jack and drilling rig south of Midland, Texas.

item.title: Diesel Crisis Puts Poor Farmers Around the World at Risk
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has limited the shipment of diesel, depriving farmers of fuel for irrigation pumps and threatening the food supply.
item.dc:creator: Peter S. Goodman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Fabeha Monir for The New York Times

item.title: U.S. Employers Forced to Fire Thousands of Immigrants, and a Showdown over Peptides
item.description: Plus, America says no thanks to salad.
item.dc:creator: Tracy Mumford, Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas and Ian Stewart
item.media:credit: Ian C. Bates for The New York Times
item.media:description: More than half of 400 U.S. nursing homes surveyed in 2023 said they had turned away prospective residents because of a lack of staff.

item.title: Oil-Rich Saudi Arabia Isn’t Short on Energy. Here’s Why It’s Going Nuclear.
item.description: The world’s largest exporter of crude oil stands to benefit from diversifying its energy sources — and freeing up more oil to sell.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: For South Korea, U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Exposes a Double Standard
item.description: President Trump has put Saudi Arabia on the path to enriching uranium on its own soil, a demand South Korea has been making for years.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.media:description: A beach near Wolseong Nuclear Power Plant in Gyeongju, South Korea, in 2022. The country has one of the biggest nuclear power industries in the world.

item.title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
item.description: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn
item.media:description: Cooling towers at the nuclear reactor facility at the Alvin W. Vogtle Electric Generating Plant in Waynesboro, Ga.

item.title: Google Hit With $1 Billion Fine By EU Over Search Engine Practices
item.description: At a tense moment for trans-Atlantic trade, the European Union accused Google of anti-competitive business practices.
item.dc:creator: Adam Satariano and Jeanna Smialek
item.media:description: Regulators said Google unfairly boosted its services in areas like shopping, travel and games, displaying its own business lines more prominently at the top of search results.

item.title: China Rewrites the ‘Soft Power’ Playbook for the A.I. Age
item.description: China is seeking global influence and good will with its open, low-cost artificial intelligence software.
item.dc:creator: Steve Lohr
item.media:description: Moonshot’s booth at the World Artificial Intelligence Conference in Shanghai last week. Moonshot and other Chinese tech companies have championed open-source A.I.

item.title: Google Sets the Pace on Hefty A.I. Spending
item.description: The technology giant again raised its projections for investing in artificial intelligence. Many on Wall Street still worry about the eventual payoff.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly, Brian O’Keefe, Ian Mount and Theodore Schleifer
item.category: Beijing Moonshot AI Technology Co Ltd
item.media:description: Google raised its forecast for capital expenditures for the year, despite investor anxiety over tech giants’ spending.

item.title: A Peptide Showdown: F.D.A. Scientists May Clash With R.F.K.’s Agenda
item.description: Agency scientists are advising against allowing widespread use of several peptide injections. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., the health secretary, has said people should be free to experiment.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Dana G. Smith
item.media:description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. before a House Subcommittee on Health on Capitol Hill in April.

item.title: The Devotion of Willow Smith
item.description: The daughter of a Hollywood power couple, she has gone above and beyond to prove herself. Virtuosity and spiritual aspirations fill her latest, “The Thread.”
item.dc:creator: Jon Pareles

item.title: A New Target for Ukraine’s Long-Range Attacks: Russia’s Version of Amazon
item.description: Kyiv is escalating its air war by striking commercial warehouses, trying to bring the conflict closer to home for ordinary Russians.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Hopkins and Alina Lobzina
item.media:description: Smoke rising from a fire at a Wildberries logistics complex in Elektrostal, near Moscow, on July 18, after a Ukrainian drone attack.

item.title: Zelensky’s Dizzying U-Turn Is the Latest Zigzag in a Presidency Full of Them
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine has pivoted from unpopular moves before, but a recent decision about military leadership and strategy could weaken his position.
item.dc:creator: Andrew E. Kramer
item.media:description: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine during a visit to Paris this month. His country is in the grips of some of the worst turbulence in domestic politics since Russia’s all-out invasion in 2022.

item.title: ‘We Will All Die From Ebola Here’: Virus Stalks Crowded Camps in Congo
item.description: There are a million people displaced by violence in the epicenter. They live in dangerous conditions and are fighting the virus with very little clean water.
item.dc:creator: Finbarr O’Reilly
item.media:credit: Finbarr O'Reilly for The New York Times

item.title: Saying ‘Google It’ Makes You Sound Old. ‘Search It Up’ Instead.
item.description: As online research evolves, so has the language we use to describe it.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Haigney

item.title: How to Use ChatGPT and Gemini Prompts to Find Out What They Know About You
item.description: It can be unsettling what Gemini and ChatGPT have figured out about you and how easily your privacy can be punctured. Here’s how to find out.
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen

item.title: F.D.A. Announces New Cyclosporiasis Outbreak With 72 Cases
item.description: Health officials did not identify the location of the outbreak, or what they believed was causing it.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi

item.title: Amid Cyclospora Outbreak Tied to Lettuce, America Skips Salads
item.description: Foot traffic to chains such as Chopt and Chipotle fell in recent weeks after a recall from Taylor Farms and changing messages from the F.D.A.
item.dc:creator: Julie Creswell
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: A worker preparing an order at a Chopt restaurant in Manhattan.

item.title: Japan’s Leader Invokes Her Hero, Shinzo Abe, to Drive Nationalist Agenda
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is leveraging her ties to Mr. Abe, the slain Japanese leader, as she pushes to reshape defense and the economy.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.media:credit: Kentaro Takahashi for The New York Times
item.media:description: Sanae Takaichi has frequently leveraged her ties to Shinzo Abe.

item.title: A Russian Spy, Suddenly Cast Into the Spotlight, Flees Japan
item.description: The military intelligence officer left the country after The Times reported that he was using the Aeroflot airline office in Tokyo to conduct espionage.
item.dc:creator: Michael Schwirtz, Adam Goldman and Jane Bradley
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Toranomon Kotohira Tower is home to the Aeroflot airline office in Tokyo.

item.title: Four Young Mathematicians Receive Prestigious Fields Medals
item.description: The awards are bestowed only once every four years to mathematicians under the age of 40.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

item.title: Gen Z Fell Out of Love With Work. I Don’t Blame Them.
item.description: For Gen Z college graduates, the way millennials lived out their ambitions no longer makes sense.
item.dc:creator: Emi Nietfeld
item.media:credit: Photo illustration by Rosie Clements for The New York Times

item.title: Iran, China, Canada? A Defense of Trump’s Grand Strategy
item.description: A Pentagon official argues that “people can no longer take the United States for granted.”
item.dc:creator: Ross Douthat and Sophia Alvarez Boyd
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Photographic Evidence
item.description: We look at how The Times uses photographs and video to better understand what’s happening in the war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Late Night Rags on Trump and His Cabinet Joining TikTok
item.description: “Right now TikTok is, like, ‘Oh, no, are we the new Facebook?’” Jimmy Fallon said on “The Tonight Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: ‘The Odyssey’ Hired Their Viking Ship. They Say It Came Back Damaged.
item.description: A Swedish Viking center said its vessel was used in the filming of Christopher Nolan’s epic but needed repairs when it was returned. Now, the center is seeking compensation.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall
